etymology.description: semantic 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") + phonetic 己(己) (jǐ) -> record
etymology.interpretation: The character 紀(紀) (jì, "record") combines 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread"), symbolizing the idea of threads or lines, with 己(己) (jǐ), which provides the phonetic element. Together, they form the concept of recording or chronicling, akin to weaving threads into a coherent narrative or account.
etymology.simplification: 紀(紀) was simplified to 纪(紀) by changing 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") to its simpler counterpart 纟, reducing stroke count.

examples.chinese: 我们把每年的活动都记录下来(我們把每年的活動都記錄下來)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bǎ měinián de huódòng dōu jìlù xiàlái.
examples.english: We record all the activities of each year.

examples.chinese: 历史书中记录了许多重要的事件(歷史書中記錄了許多重要的事件)。
examples.pinyin: Lìshǐ shū zhōng jìlùle xǔduō zhòngyào de shìjiàn.
examples.english: Many important events are chronicled in history books.

examples.chinese: 他的生平被细致地记录在这本传记中(他的生平被細緻地記錄在這本傳記中)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de shēngpíng bèi xìzhì de jìlù zài zhè běn zhuànjì zhōng.
examples.english: His life is meticulously recorded in this biography.
